title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brought significant improvements to automation reliability and system performance. OpsPal now automatically detects and fixes missing dependencies at startup, preventing workflow interruptions. Additionally, enhanced Salesforce integration ensures smoother data flow creation and proper permission handling.

entries.headline: Salesforce workflows now validate permissions and context properly
details: Flow creation and editing now includes proper permission checking
Automated permission assignment prevents access errors

entries.headline: Enhanced compliance monitoring for automation workflows
details: Workflows now validate against security policies automatically
Better enforcement of data handling requirements

entries.headline: System maintenance now runs automatically during updates
details: OpsPal cleans up old files and sessions automatically
Warnings appear when system memory usage gets high

entries.headline: Salesforce form and permission management works more reliably
details: Dynamic Forms detection prevents field visibility issues
New record types automatically get proper permissions
Workflow routing no longer blocks sessions unexpectedly

entries.headline: OpsPal automatically fixes missing dependencies at startup
details: System detects and installs missing components automatically
Clear error messages show when manual fixes are needed

entries.headline: Resolved false dependency warnings during startup
